name: Web - HTTP 5xx Error Spike (Application Attack Indicator)
description: Detects spikes in HTTP 5xx server error responses which can indicate an active attack overwhelming the application, a successful exploit causing crashes, or misconfigured attack tools triggering application errors. Useful as a secondary indicator alongside other rules.
search: index=web (sourcetype=nginx_access OR sourcetype=apache_access) status>=500 status<600 | bucket _time span=5m | stats count as error_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, dc(uri) as unique_uris, values(status) as error_codes by host, _time | where error_count >= 50 | eval error_rate_indicator=case( error_count >= 500, "Critical spike - possible active exploit", error_count >= 200, "High spike - investigate immediately", error_count >= 50, "Elevated errors - monitor closely") | eval possible_cause=case( unique_ips <= 3, "Single source - likely targeted attack", unique_ips <= 20, "Small group - coordinated attack possible", unique_ips > 20, "Distributed - DDoS or mass scan") | eval severity=case( error_count >= 500, "high", error_count >= 200, "medium", 1==1, "low") | eval mitre="T1499" | table _time, host, error_count, unique_ips, unique_uris, error_codes, error_rate_indicator, possible_cause, severity, mitre
author: SOC Team
